outages.headline: Venezuelan crude fills the feedstock gap left by Middle Eastern disruption
outages.summary: The Strait of Hormuz closure severed a critical supply line for US Gulf Coast refineries — not just of crude oil, but of the heavy residual material their specialist processing units need to maximise diesel output. Venezuelan crude has stepped in to fill both roles, and is likely to remain strategically important even after the ...
